title: centaurea alba subsp. formanekii
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. formanekii, also known as White Knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to Central Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of White Knapweed include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases. White Knapweed can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and address various health concerns.
